Estimate season random effect causally from accumulated observations

Source: R/m2_training.R
estimate_season_re_online.Rd

Computes a shrinkage scalar for the current season's intercept shift on the logit scale using only observations up to the current week. The estimate equals the mean logit-scale residual (observed minus GAM prediction without season RE), shrunk toward zero by lambda_re. Adding this scalar to bias_logit in m2_predict_one() replaces the hard exclusion of s(season) while remaining causally safe.

Usage

estimate_season_re_online(fit, obs_df, ex_terms = NULL, lambda_re = 1)

Arguments

fit

Fitted mgcv GAM with a season random effect.

obs_df

Data frame of current-season observations with columns y, N, and all covariates required by fit.

ex_terms

Character vector of additional terms to exclude.

lambda_re

Shrinkage strength; default 1 (one pseudo-observation at 0).

Value

Numeric scalar (the shrinkage RE estimate on the logit scale).

On this page
